nativeRange.habitat: Deep reef drop-offs where gorgonians and black corals are abundant; typically found below 40 m depth, though shallower in some East Indian locations

diet.notes: Has been documented accepting frozen and freeze-dried diets containing algae, supplemented with dried algae, vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, and mysis shrimp. In the wild tends to feed on gorgonians, black corals, and associated invertebrates at depth.

imageCandidates.notes: Live aquarium specimen, 2816x2112. Shows the diagnostic Burgess' Butterflyfish pattern: white body with broad black diagonal bands, black eye band and black upper-rear/dorsal; whole fish in frame and in focus. Slight blue cast/glass haze but pattern is crisp.

summary: A shy deep-water butterflyfish from the Western Pacific, generally considered intermediate to keep due to its preference for dimmer conditions and live-rock-rich environments. Not reef-safe with corals or anemones.
careNotes: Burgess' Butterflyfish tends to be quite shy and has been reported to do best when provided with multiple hiding places — dense live rock with caves and overhangs is generally advised. Because this species is naturally found at depth (commonly 40–130 m), it tends to prefer somewhat dimmer lighting and areas with cover; a tank with varied light zones is recommended. It is generally not considered reef-safe, as it has been known to pick at feather dusters, anemones, and LPS corals. It can be kept singly or as a mated pair; this species is regarded as monogamous and, when kept in pairs, does best when introduced as an already-bonded pair rather than two unacquainted individuals. Aquarists should monitor individual response and adjust accordingly.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (14 cm TL — agrees with LiveAquaria 5.5 in), native range, taxonomy, and temperature. Temperature disagreement: FishBase wild data 25.7–28.7°C vs LiveAquaria 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C); averaged to 24–27°C.
